title: Adam he said or she said
display_title: Adam he said or she said
wikitext:
[]: Adam... he said or she said?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 15, 2014 10:09 pm Split from the thread "Is there a deeper reason we pick our ships?", HERE . Anything from NGE that I might be considered a "shipper" of is so weird / obscure that, yes, it probably does say something about me, though I'm not precisely sure what.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Wed Oct 15, 2014 10:11 pm ^ Oh, come on! You can't post something like that and then skimp on the details!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 15, 2014 10:36 pm Gob Hobblin wrote: Oh, come on! You can't post something like that and then skimp on the details!

[]:             Quote Post by Shamsiel-kun » Thu Oct 16, 2014 5:31 am Reichu wrote: Well, back when I was writing (never-finished) fanfic about the Evas and Angels, I had these crack pairings going: - Eva-01/Zeruel - Eva-02/Sachiel - Eva-00/Eva-03 ...but I since exported whatever the hell I was doing there into an original setting where nothing annoying like canon can come between the love that giant flat-chested women and bizarro daikaijuu feel for one another. For a storyline I haven't completely given up on, I've taken to shipping Dr. "Akira" Katsuragi with both Adam and (post-3I) Kyoko. You may wonder about the logistics of the first one, but it makes (slightly) more sense in context. And since that context is still trapped in my head, no one has any chance of understanding what the hell I'm thinking unless they listen to me rant for at least an hour. Probably for the best, since I've been writing Adam as explicitly female, and the Eva fandom will never accept that aside from a small handful of misfits.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Oct 16, 2014 7:56 am You people are just asking for trouble. Well, it's amusing, anyway, so might as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Thu Oct 16, 2014 8:41 am Please, Bagheera: do share. Kendrix, I had time to reread your post and saw a lot of good points in it. I want to discuss some of them, but that'll have to wait until I get to a keyboard. But for now, I do want to point out that it's got me thinking.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Oct 16, 2014 9:33 am Gob Hobblin wrote: Please, Bagheera: do share.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Oct 16, 2014 9:40 am Reichu wrote: I've been writing Adam as explicitly female, and the Eva fandom will never accept that aside from a small handful of misfits.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Oct 16, 2014 9:49 am Mr. Tines wrote: How is this even controversial when this is one of the things that's directly stated

[]:             Quote Post by Shamsiel-kun » Thu Oct 16, 2014 12:40 pm Bagheera wrote: Because Adam and Kaworu. Also, "mother" and "female" have a more complex relationship than is commonly assumed. A "mother ship" is not female, for instance, nor even alive (though the Japanese term in question might be explicitly feminine, I don't know).

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Oct 16, 2014 12:50 pm Shamsiel-kun wrote: But a "mother ship" generally does carry one or more smaller vessels, therefore meeting one of the requirements for a mother.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Thu Oct 16, 2014 12:51 pm Nuclear Lunchbox wrote: I do admit, shipping characters does become a lot easier when I feel some sort of affection for them myself..

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Oct 16, 2014 1:46 pm Mr. Tines wrote: How is this even controversial when this is one of the things that's directly stated

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Oct 16, 2014 1:59 pm Reichu wrote: Anyway, much as I'm tempted to derail the conversation over the unexpected interest in my weird head-canons, I'm afraid it would only be tangentially on topic at best, so I'll refrain.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Oct 16, 2014 2:02 pm I thought everyone here calls Adam she, and do so myself on the rare occasions I have a need to.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Oct 16, 2014 2:05 pm pwhodges wrote: I thought everyone here calls Adam she, and do so myself on the rare occasions I have a need to.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Oct 16, 2014 6:35 pm I've just always gone with a male Adam and a female Lilith-- although yes, the sex has been questioned many times. It's just my personal preference to do so, having never been faced with a concrete "You are wrong".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Oct 16, 2014 6:47 pm I'm sorry I started all this by not just ignoring Gob Hobblin's (joking?) desire for "details". Can the thread go back to its previously scheduled programming now?

[]:             Quote Post by Gob Hobblin » Thu Oct 16, 2014 8:05 pm It was a joking demand, but I found the derailment amusing and enlightening. It certainly provided me a perspective of Adam (and therefore Kaworu) I never had before.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Oct 16, 2014 8:33 pm We also have a magic mod who splits threads for a mod-living.
